abstract.t: This document defines the "charge" payment intent for use with the Payment HTTP Authentication Scheme . The "charge" intent represents a one-time payment where the payer provides proof of payment immediately in exchange for resource access.

section.name: Introduction
section.t: The "charge" intent is the most fundamental payment pattern: a one-time exchange of payment for resource access. The payer provides proof of payment (or a signed authorization to collect payment), and the server grants access to the requested resource.
This intent applies to any payment method that supports immediate payment verification, including:

section.name: Relationship to Payment Methods
section.t: This document defines the abstract semantics of the "charge" intent. Payment method specifications define how to implement this intent using their specific payment infrastructure.

section.name: Requirements Language
section.t: The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT", "SHOULD", "SHOULD NOT", "RECOMMENDED", "NOT RECOMMENDED", "MAY", and "OPTIONAL" in this document are to be interpreted as described in BCP 14 when, and only when, they appear in all capitals, as shown here.

section.name: Definition
section.t: The "charge" intent represents a request for immediate, one-time payment of a specified amount in exchange for resource access.

ol.li: Server issues a 402 response with intent="charge"
Client submits credential with proof of payment
Server verifies payment and grants access

section.name: Atomicity
section.t: The "charge" intent implies atomic exchange: the server SHOULD NOT provide partial access if payment verification fails. Either the full resource is provided (payment succeeded) or access is denied (payment failed).

section.name: Request Schema
section.t: The request parameter for a "charge" intent MUST include sufficient information for the client to complete payment. At minimum, payment method specifications MUST define:

section.name: Example
section.t: Payment method specifications define the complete schema for their implementation of the "charge" intent.

section.name: Payload
section.t: The credential payload for a "charge" intent MUST contain proof that payment has been made or authorized. The proof type is method-specific:

section.name: Single-Use
section.t: Each credential MUST be usable only once per challenge. Servers MUST reject replayed credentials.

section.name: Server Responsibilities
section.t: Servers verifying a "charge" credential MUST:

ol.li: Verify the id matches an outstanding challenge
Verify the challenge has not expired
Verify the payment proof using method-specific procedures
Verify the payment amount matches the request
Verify the payment recipient matches the request

section.name: Settlement
section.t: Settlement semantics are method-specific:

ul.li: Immediate settlement: Payment is final upon verification

section.t: (e.g., Lightning preimage, confirmed blockchain transaction)

ul.li: Deferred settlement: Server submits payment after verification

section.t: (e.g., signed authorization submitted to chain)

ul.li: Processor settlement: External processor handles settlement

section.t: (e.g., Stripe PaymentIntent)

section.name: Amount Verification
section.t: Clients MUST verify the requested amount is appropriate for the resource before authorizing payment. Malicious servers could request excessive amounts.

section.name: Recipient Verification
section.t: Clients SHOULD verify the payment recipient when possible. For methods that support recipient verification (e.g., known merchant addresses), clients SHOULD warn users about unknown recipients.

section.name: Replay Protection
section.t: Servers MUST implement replay protection. Each challenge id MUST be single-use. Servers MUST NOT accept the same credential twice.

section.name: Finality
section.t: The finality of a "charge" payment depends on the payment method:

ul.li: Some methods may have delayed finality (blockchain confirmations)
Some methods may be reversible (card chargebacks)

section.t: Servers SHOULD understand the finality guarantees of their accepted payment methods and adjust resource access accordingly.

section.name: Payment Intent Registration
section.t: This document registers the "charge" intent in the "HTTP Payment Intents" registry established by :
